findings.title: Greedy brace JSON extraction risks malformed payload injection
findings.rationale: Strategy 3 (first { → last }) can capture non-JSON text, nested structures, or truncated LLM outputs. Downstream consumers may parse invalid data or write unsafe files if validation is skipped.
findings.remediation: Add strict JSON schema validation post-extraction; limit scan depth; prefer incremental/streaming JSON parser; fallback to safe error state on mismatch.

findings.title: Symlink/TOCTOU bypass in resolve()+is_relative_to path defense
findings.rationale: Path.resolve() follows symlinks. An attacker can place a symlink inside worktree pointing outside, passing is_relative_to check but writing outside. Race conditions possible between check and write.
findings.remediation: Use os.path.realpath() consistently; verify no symlinks cross worktree boundary; perform atomic writes with O_NOFOLLOW or use directory FDs (openat).

findings.title: PYTHONPATH prepending '.' and 'src' enables module shadowing
findings.rationale: Prepending current directory and src to PYTHONPATH risks importing malicious/unintended modules over stdlib or dependencies if names collide, especially in untrusted workdirs.
findings.remediation: Remove '.' from PYTHONPATH; enforce explicit absolute imports; isolate phase_3 scoring in a clean subprocess with strictly controlled sys.path or dedicated venv.

findings.title: Blanket stdin=DEVNULL may break subprocesses expecting input
findings.rationale: Applying DEVNULL to 12 subprocess sites prevents hanging but will crash or silently fail tools that legitimately read stdin (e.g., git hooks, formatters, or piped data consumers).
findings.remediation: Audit each subprocess for stdin requirements; use asyncio.subprocess.PIPE only where input is needed; keep DEVNULL for strictly non-interactive calls.

findings.title: Monolithic OpenRouterAdapter lacks family isolation & circuit breaking
findings.rationale: Routing 5 distinct Chinese model families through a single adapter without per-family rate limits, quotas, or fallbacks risks cascading failures and quota exhaustion under load.
findings.remediation: Implement per-family circuit breakers, distinct rate-limit buckets, and retry/backoff policies; add graceful degradation to alternative families/providers.

findings.title: fixer_template.format(workdir) vulnerable to brace collision & injection
findings.rationale: str.format() crashes if workdir contains unescaped '{' or '}'. If workdir is attacker-controlled or contains special chars, it can break rendering or enable prompt injection.
findings.remediation: Switch to string.Template or Jinja2 with auto-escaping; sanitize/quote workdir paths; validate placeholder injection strictly before formatting.

findings.title: Missing test harness for new JSON extractor & adapter routing
findings.rationale: No test strategy described for _json_extract.py's 3 strategies, path traversal defenses, or OpenRouter family routing. Edge cases (nested braces, malformed markdown, API timeouts) are unverified.
findings.remediation: Add property-based tests (Hypothesis) for JSON extraction; mock OpenRouter responses per family; assert path traversal defenses with symlink/TOCTOU simulations.

findings.title: Global skill installation breaks reproducibility & permission boundaries
findings.rationale: Placing SKILL.md in ~/.claude/skills/ is user-global, not repo-scoped, breaking CI/reproducibility. Lacks explicit sandbox/permission constraints for agent execution.
findings.remediation: Ship skill in-repo (e.g., .claude/skills/ or docs/skills/); document explicit allow/deny rules for filesystem/network access; version-lock skill with repo.
